name: Leonard Cohen
bio: Leonard Cohen's catalog remains valuable through songwriter-led economics, deep catalog listening, and songs that continue to circulate across covers, streaming, and sync use.
notable_for: One of the strongest writer-driven catalogs in folk and literary songwriting.
career_highlight: Hallelujah remains one of the clearest examples of a song whose long-tail value extends far beyond its original recording cycle.
revenue_drivers: Songwriter-led catalog economics preserve strong retained value.
Covers, reinterpretations, and sync use extend long-tail earnings.
Folk and reflective playlist listening support recurring discovery.

earnings.assumptions: Estimate keeps Leonard Cohen's headline range as the artist-side figure and models gross catalog, label, publishing, and writer lanes from that conservative annual range.

ownership.master_owner: Likely split across label, distributor, and artist-affiliated rights depending on recording era
ownership.publishing_owner: Writer and publisher splits materially affect final artist-side income
ownership.catalog_sale_status: No full catalog sale assumption baked into this modeled range
ownership.notes: Leonard Cohen's page should be read as modeled artist-side annual income, not a public royalty statement. Ownership and label terms can materially change take-home economics.
